# Grant — Player Profile

**Callsign:** Grant | **Tracker:** Eh_Grant | **Role:** IGL / Flex | **Email:** grantgreenfelder@gmail.com

---

## Identity

**Strengths:** Best gun in the stack. Calm under pressure. Strong map knowledge and experience. Quick to adapt. Natural leader — the voice everyone listens to. Excels in solo positions and controlling his own situation.

**Tendencies:** Plays best when in full control of his own actions. Doesn't need frag assistance. Comfortable in isolated, high-pressure moments. Highest clutch win% on the team — composure is a consistent trait, not a season fluke.

**Role fit:** Primary IGL. Solo pusher. Flex operator. Backside/rotational disruption during executes.

**Solo vs. Group:** #1 priority for solo roles in any formation.

---

## Career Coaching Notes

- **Best player on the team** by K/D and clutch metrics across tracked seasons. No structural weaknesses.
- **Chalet** is a recurring team-wide problem — not Grant-specific. Don't assign individual blame.
- Low HS% relative to teammates (mid-30s%) is consistent with his calm, cerebral aim style — not a weakness to correct.
- IGL role is appropriate and earns the stack its best outcomes. Don't pull him into rigid formed roles that limit his reads.

# Grant — Y11S1 Coaching Brief

_Season: Year 11 Season 1 — Operation Silent Hunt | Updated: 2026-03-07_

---

## Your Role

You're the IGL and the best gun on the team. Your job is to make the calls, win the solo duels, and get into positions nobody else can handle on their own. The team plays around you — your reads are the plan, your clutches are the safety net. Don't overcomplicate it.

---

## What's Working

**Operators — DEF**
Your Kapkan/Thorn trap anchor formula was elite in Y10S4 — Kapkan at 95r / 62.1% WR / 1.75 K/D, Thorn close behind at 90r / 65.6% WR / 1.65 K/D. That's locked in. Don't change it.

**Operators — ATK**
Your Amaru identity is real (78r / 52.6% / 1.38 K/D in Y10S4) and got a free buff in Y11S1 — you now have 5 Garra Hook charges instead of 4. Lion and Capitão are solid secondary picks.

**Maps**
Your all-time strong maps are Villa (64.3%), Bank (58.1%), and Fortress/Oregon (both 54.5%). In Y10S4 specifically: Oregon was your best at 71.4% with a 1.96 K/D, and Lair was 75.0%. Both maps stay in the pool for the first half of Y11S1.

---

## Areas to Work On

**Operators — ATK**
Your Y11S1 opening data shows Lion leading at 20r with 40.0% WR and 0.69 K/D — that's a weak start. Thatcher and Capitão are outperforming in smaller samples. Watch whether to shift back toward Amaru-first as the season develops. Don't force Lion if the data keeps pointing down.

**Operators — Utility**
Osa posted 32.3% WR over 31 rounds in Y10S4. If Osa is creeping back into your pool this season, evaluate whether the team is actually capitalizing on the shield presence for plants. If not, drop it.

**Maps — ATK side**
Kanal is out of the pool for the first half, which removes a pain point — but Nighthaven (27.3% all-time) and Chalet (31.8% all-time) are still in play. Nighthaven in particular is your worst career map. Have a plan for it.

---

## Y11S1 Priorities

**1. Protect your best maps in ban phase.**
Oregon stays all first half (your 71.4% / 1.96 K/D map in Y10S4). Villa stays all first half too. These are your two most dominant maps right now — do not give them up in the ban phase if you can help it.

**2. Thatcher is your Y11S1 ATK identity.**
At 57r / 49.1% / 1.25 K/D, Thatcher has become your highest-volume ATK op this season. Amaru is still in the pool and performing (24r / 45.8% / 1.38), but the data says Thatcher is leading right now. Let the volume keep building — if Thatcher stays above 45% WR, that's your primary. Amaru is the flex pick, not the anchor.

**3. Observation Blocker deploys in 1 second now.**
Kaid is in your all-time DEF top 5. Any op you play that carries the Observation Blocker — place it in prep phase every round. The cost is now negligible.

---

_Detailed operator tables, map splits, and full season archives are in your ALL_TIME.md, Y10S4.md, and Y11S1.md files._

mainStack.coachingContent:
# Peej — Y11S1 Coaching Brief

_Season: Year 11 Season 1 — Operation Silent Hunt | Updated: 2026-03-07_

---

## Your Role

You're the stack's entry fragger — the role the team needed and the one you've earned. Ash at 1,205 career rounds is not a gimmick, it's a career commitment. Your DEF side (Maestro/Caveira) posts elite WR and anchors the team's defensive structure. Y11S1 is the season to build on everything that Y10S4 established.

---

## What's Working

**Operators — DEF**
Maestro and Caveira are your anchors and they're elite — 70.5% and 66.0% WR in Y10S4 respectively, over 53r and 61r. Those are among the best individual DEF op WRs on the roster. Caveira (62.6% WR / 1.42 K/D all-time) and Aruni (63.9% WR / 1.39 K/D all-time) round out a genuinely strong DEF profile.

**Operators — ATK**
Ash is your identity (1,205 all-time rounds). Brava is the complement (45.8% WR / 1.31 K/D all-time). Both ops suit independent, aggressive action — which is your playstyle. In Y10S4, Ash held 1.00 K/D at the season's end.

**Maps**
Your top maps are Bank (56.7%), Border (55.2%), Clubhouse (54.8%), and Villa (57.9%) all-time. Bank and Clubhouse are in the pool all first half. Villa is in first half too. Theme Park (58.3%) and Skyscraper (60.0%) are your other strong maps — they're out for the first half but return mid-season.

---

## Areas to Work On

**Maps — First half**
Your two best career maps (Skyscraper and Theme Park) are both out until the mid-season swap. That means the first half depends more on Bank, Clubhouse, and Villa. Be deliberate about those three — they're your best available options and you post good numbers on all of them.

**Maps — Permanent avoids**
Coastline (20.0% WR / 15M) and Nighthaven (23.1% / 13M) are consistent career weaknesses across enough matches to be high confidence. Coastline is in the pool first half — have a plan for it or advocate to ban it. Nighthaven is out first half, which is a break.

**Operators — Ying**
In Y10S4, Ying was your third ATK op at 35r but only 34.3% WR. That's below the rest of your pool's efficiency. Ash/Brava should lead in rounds, and if a third ATK op is needed, Twitch (57.1% WR in 7r) and Dokkaebi (71.4% WR in 7r) both outperform Ying in smaller Y10S4 samples.

---

## Y11S1 Priorities

**1. Protect Bank in ban phase.**
Bank is your most consistent strong map with a solid 30-game sample (56.7% all-time). It's in the pool all season. When ban discussions happen, Bank should be the map you're keeping, not offering.

**2. Maestro/Caveira Observation Blocker is now 1-second deployment.**
Both ops carry it. Place it early in prep phase every round — the cost is now effectively zero. It denies drone intel passively and it's free value. Build it as a habit immediately.

**3. You're the entry fragger now — own it.**
54M into Y11S1 and you're the second-highest RIS on the stack (56.7). Ash (40r) leads your ATK pool and Caveira (53r / 58.5%) is your DEF anchor. The role is confirmed. Focus on coordinating Ash entry with breach timing — if you're dying on entry, ask whether hard breach went first.

---

_Detailed operator tables, map splits, and full season archives are in your ALL_TIME.md, Y10S4.md, and Y11S1.md files._

# Hound — Y11S1 Coaching Brief

_Season: Year 11 Season 1 — Operation Silent Hunt | Updated: 2026-03-07_

---

## Your Role

You're the team's group support. On attack, you provide breach and gadget clearance — Thatcher is your identity because it enables everyone else to do their job. On defense, you hold site with utility and coordination. Your contribution is measured in team wins, not personal frags, and the numbers back that up. Stay grouped. Stay patient.

---

## What's Working

**Operators — ATK**
Thatcher is your career ATK anchor at 729 all-time rounds — the most committed Thatcher on the main stack. It works because it enables the group. Rauora is now a confirmed ATK pick and you're getting real results with it (58.3% WR / 36 rounds in Y10S4). Gridlock is an underrated depth pick: 51.9% WR and 1.47 K/D in 27 Y10S4 rounds.

**Operators — DEF**
Your Y10S4 DEF pool was carrying. Frost (70.0% WR) and Aruni (77.8% WR / 2.22 K/D) were your two most efficient individual DEF ops. Castle held the main anchor volume at 81 rounds / 64.2% WR. Kaid in small samples (80.0% WR / 1.78 K/D in 15r) was outstanding.

**Maps**
Your best maps in Y10S4 were Villa (85.7%), Fortress (72.7%), and Clubhouse (66.7%). All-time, Theme Park (65.2%) and Kafe (61.2%) are consistent. Several of these are in the current Y11S1 pool.

---

## Areas to Work On

**Operators — DEF: Frost vs. Castle**
Castle leads your DEF by rounds, but Frost outperforms it on every individual metric — better WR, better K/D. The coaching question is whether your Castle usage is intentional coordination strategy (barricade callouts, coordinated slow), or just comfort. If it's coordination-based, lean into it explicitly and make sure the team knows when to expect it. If it's comfort, consider giving Frost more volume.

**Operators — ATK**
Your ATK K/Ds are low by design — you're not the fragger in the group, and that's correct. The one op to watch is Buck (31r / 35.5% WR in Y10S4). If Buck is getting played without a clear vertical or soft-breach purpose, consider whether it's the right call or whether Thatcher/Rauora would serve better in those rounds.

**Role reminder — solo entry**
This is a standing instruction across all seasons: never take solo entry duels. Your gun is not the team's best in a standalone 1v1. Always be grouped. Always enable. The data confirms this approach wins.

---

## Y11S1 Priorities

**1. Stay grouped — every time.**
Solo roles belong to Grant and Peej (Slug B Team as of 2026-03-21). Your value comes from the group. When you're in a 3-man with Smigs and Sarge, the team's synergy is at its best. Resist any impulse to go off on your own.

**2. Frost deserves more rounds than it's currently getting.**
In Y10S4 it was your second-best DEF op by WR (70.0%). You can play both Castle and Frost in the same match — they serve different purposes. Give Frost more opportunity this season and track whether the WR gap over Castle holds.

**3. Gridlock and Rauora are real.**
Gridlock (51.9% WR / 1.47 K/D in 27r) and Rauora (58.3% WR in 36r) are both producing. If you've been playing these and the team hasn't fully incorporated the area denial or the flank cover they provide, bring it up. These ops add team value beyond your individual frags.

---

_Detailed operator tables, map splits, and full season archives are in your ALL_TIME.md, Y10S4.md, and Y11S1.md files._

# Smigs — Y11S1 Coaching Brief

_Season: Year 11 Season 1 — Operation Silent Hunt | Updated: 2026-03-07_

---

## Your Role

You're the "sir yes sir" of this team — the most reliable follower in a coordinated push. That's not a knock, it's the highest compliment for a group-entry player. You do what the team needs without hesitation, and your win rate consistently reflects it. Your DEF identity as a zone-control anchor (Kaid/Aruni/Castle) is one of the most stable on the stack.

---

## What's Working

**Operators — DEF**
Your zone-control anchor formula is already confirmed active in Y11S1: Aruni (71.4% WR) and Kaid (57.1% WR) both in the top 2 early. In Y10S4, Aruni was your best DEF fragger at 1.60 K/D in 49r. That combination of zone denial and actual fragging is rare — own it.

**Operators — ATK**
Thermite is your ATK anchor (81r / 49.4% WR / 1.11 K/D in Y10S4). That's solid utility volume. Capitão was your standout last season at 62.5% WR in 24 rounds — the highest ATK WR in your pool. Nomad is producing early in Y11S1 (80% WR in 5r — tiny sample, but positive directional signal).

**Maps**
Your three best maps right now are Border (77.8% in Y10S4), Oregon (71.4% in Y10S4), and Lair (71.4% all-time). Border and Oregon are both in the current pool for the first half.

---

## Areas to Work On

**Operators — ATK**
Capitão needs more volume. You played 24 rounds at 62.5% WR and then it didn't grow further. That's the highest ATK WR in your pool — give it more chances. If Osa and Striker are eating rounds ahead of Capitão, that's the wrong call.

**Maps**
Clubhouse is your biggest map weakness: 41.2% WR in 51 career matches. That's too many games to dismiss as variance. It's your most reliable bad map by sample size. When the team is building a ban strategy, Clubhouse should be in the conversation. Chalet (40.9%) and Outback (39.3%) are also consistent weak spots.

**Operators — DEF: Lesion is underplayed**
In Y10S4, Lesion posted 59.3% WR and 1.67 K/D in 27 rounds — your most efficient DEF fragger next to Aruni. That's a 60%+ WR op with real kill potential and you only played it 27 times. It deserves a look for maps where Kaid/Castle aren't the right call.

---

## Y11S1 Priorities

**1. Give Capitão more ATK rounds.**
It was your best ATK op last season and nothing in your pool challenges it at that WR. If you're defaulting to Osa or Striker when Capitão is available, flip that priority.

**2. Kaid Observation Blocker deploys in 1 second now.**
Your DEF anchor op carries it. There is no longer a good reason not to place it in prep phase every round. Put it down, every time. It denies drone info and costs you nothing.

**3. Win rate is still the right metric for you.**

# Sarge — Player Profile

**Callsign:** Sarge | **Tracker:** Eh_SiegeyBodeez | **Role:** Entry / Fragger

⚠️ **Callsign note:** Some old script files and earlier KB docs swap Smigs/Sarge. Authoritative mapping: **Sarge = Eh_SiegeyBodeez**, Smigs = Eh_tooten. Trust tracker usernames over callsign labels in any document with ambiguity.

---

## Identity

**Strengths:** One of the best guns in the stack. Good balance of aggressive and passive play. Excellent at quick entry and breaching. Can coordinate his own strike team.

**Tendencies:** Coordination similar to Grant but less composed under pressure. Excels at getting early man advantage or map control. Comfortable leading a sub-group. **Defense is his dominant side** — this is a consistent career pattern, not just a season anomaly.

**Role fit:** Entry fragger (role label) — but data consistently shows a defense-dominant identity. Best used as sub-group IGL on attack, primary anchor on defense.

**Solo vs. Group:** Most valuable leading a 2–3 man group. Solo roles should go to Grant or Peej first (Slug B Team as of 2026-03-21).

---

## Career Coaching Notes

- **Role/op mismatch is the standing coaching priority.** Called "Entry/Fragger" but his Y10S4 ATK top 5 (Thermite/Buck/IQ/Sledge/Hibana) posted sub-1.0 K/D on every operator this season. Note: all-time, three of his top 5 ATK ops have hit 1.0+ K/D (Ace 1.15, Thatcher 1.02, Sledge 1.02) — so the issue is Y10S4 op selection, not solely mechanics. Either evolve toward actual entry ops (Ash, Zofia, Iana) or update the role label.
- **Defense is his real identity.** Vigil is the career DEF anchor — all-time #1 DEF op (716r / 57.8% / 1.45 K/D, Y8S1+). Thunderbird is a strong Y10S4 addition (115r this season) but does not appear in his all-time top 5 DEF ops (Vigil/Mute/Bandit/Doc/Rook) — treat it as emerging, not career-proven. In stack play, his DEF rounds are the priority.
- **Rank gap** (historically Platinum while the rest of the stack sits at Emerald) needs a direct conversation. Solo queue vs. stack performance should be investigated.
- In stack formation: assign Sarge to lead the 3-man group on attack while Grant/Peej take solo roles (Slug B Team as of 2026-03-21). His coordination capability makes him the best choice for sub-group IGL.

# Sarge — Y11S1 Coaching Brief

_Season: Year 11 Season 1 — Operation Silent Hunt | Updated: 2026-03-07_

---

## Your Role

You're labeled Entry/Fragger, but your real identity is defense-dominant with strong sub-group leadership capability. On attack, your job is to lead the 3-man group — you're the sub-IGL, not the solo pusher. On defense, you're the team's best roamer and one of the most impactful individual DEF players on the stack. Vigil is your identity. Own it.

---

## What's Working

**Operators — DEF**
Vigil is your career anchor and it's showing up immediately in Y11S1: 31r / 58.1% / 1.60 K/D. That exactly matches your all-time Vigil numbers (716r / 57.8% / 1.45 K/D). This identity is locked in for good reason — your roaming K/D on Vigil is the highest individual defensive K/D on the main stack. Thunderbird is your backup anchor and building volume, and Azami is posting absurd early numbers (85.7% WR / 4.00 K/D in 7r).

**Operators — ATK (⭐ Major Development)**
This is a coaching win worth calling out directly: you transitioned to Zofia as your primary ATK op in Y11S1 and you have 55 rounds at 1.13 K/D. That breaks the pattern from Y10S4 where every ATK op you played was sub-1.0. Zofia is a real entry fragger (KS79 Lifeline launcher) and your K/D finally reflects an entry fragger. This is exactly the evolution the coaching notes were asking for.

**Maps**
Your three strongest maps are Theme Park (60.5% all-time), Villa (59.3% all-time), and Kafe (58.5%). Your Y10S4 best was Outback (83.3% / 1.60 K/D) but Outback is gone from the pool all season. Villa stays in for the first half — protect it.

---

## Areas to Work On

**Operators — ATK: Confirm the Zofia identity**
55 rounds is real volume, but you need the next 50 rounds to confirm the pattern holds. If the K/D and WR both stay above 1.0 and 45%+ by the time you hit 100+ rounds, you've fully made the transition. The other operators in Y11S1 (Twitch, Sledge, Buck) are all 4r or under — don't let those eat the volume that Zofia should be getting.

**Maps**
Clubhouse is your worst all-time map: 33.8% WR in 68 career matches. That's the largest and most confident weakness on the main stack. Chalet (35.3% all-time) is close behind. Both are in the current pool — have a plan for them or advocate for banning them.

**The rank gap**
You ended Y10S4 at Platinum III while most of the main stack was Emerald. Both you and Grant have reset to Silver IV in Y11S1 — that's the same starting point. The question is trajectory from here. If your rank trends toward Emerald this season, the gap is closing for real. That's the target.

---

## Y11S1 Priorities

**1. Keep building Zofia.**
You found your entry fragger identity. Don't abandon it because of a few bad rounds. The whole coaching program was pushing toward this — you got there organically. Trust it.

**2. Defense is your dominant side — lead on it.**
Your Vigil K/D (1.45 all-time) makes you one of the most impactful individual defenders on the team. In stack play, your roaming creates the pressure that lets the anchors do their job. Communicate your roam plans before the round so the anchors know where to expect traffic.

**3. Your new comfort maps are Clubhouse and Chalet — protect them.**
Y11S1 answered the Outback question: Clubhouse is 75.0% (8M) and Chalet is 62.5% (8M). Both are clear team strengths in Y11S1. Do not ban either. Border (60.0% / 10M) is the next tier. This is the map set to protect in the ban phase.

---

_Detailed operator tables, map splits, and full season archives are in your ALL_TIME.md, Y10S4.md, and Y11S1.md files._

# Slug — Y11S1 Coaching Brief

_Season: Year 11 Season 1 — Operation Silent Hunt | Updated: 2026-03-07_

---

## Your Role

You're the second IGL, the Smoke specialist, and the team's most experienced solo-queue player. You thrive operating independently — flanking, watching rotations, and making your own reads. Your post-plant Smoke denial is the most reliable on the team. You get a solo role in the team formation because your individual decision-making earns it.

---

## What's Working

**Operators — DEF**
Smoke is your career anchor — 833 all-time rounds with 58.6% WR. In Y10S4, it was your best DEF op at 128r / 64.1% WR / 1.31 K/D. Trust yourself on when to pop canisters — that's a self-directed read, and the 64% WR says it's working. Valkyrie gives the team intel behind it. This two-op DEF identity is locked in.

**Operators — ATK**
Maverick stands out as a clear best ATK pick in Y10S4 (59.1% WR / 1.79 K/D in 22r). Thatcher (50% WR / 1.35 K/D) and Jackal (61.1% WR in 18r) were also strong. These three are where you should be building ATK volume.

**Maps**
Coastline and Lair were both 83.3% in Y10S4 — those are elite numbers. Both are in the pool for the first half of Y11S1. Kanal is your third-best all-time map at 62.3%. Bank (56.3% all-time) is a consistent strength.

---

## Areas to Work On

**Operators — ATK**
Zero is the standing issue. You played it 71 rounds in Y10S4 at 38.0% WR. That's the most-played ATK op with the worst WR in the pool. You don't need to quit it entirely, but it cannot be leading your ATK pool. Buck and Thermite deserve more rounds than Zero is currently getting.

**Maps**
Chalet is your most reliable weakness — 37.4% WR in 91 career matches. That's a large enough sample to be very high confidence. Consulate is the other one: 66 career matches at 53.0% is fine all-time, but Y10S4 was 43.8% across 16 games. Specific strat work on Consulate would help.

---

## Y11S1 Priorities

**1. Zero is your ATK identity in Y11S1 — own it.**
10r / 70.0% WR in Y11S1 reverses the Y10S4 concern. Zero is performing. Keep the volume building. The Argus Cam persistence intel model suits your self-directed read style. Buck (22r / 22.7%) and Solid Snake (21r / 28.6%) are the ones to reduce — not Zero.

**2. Coastline stays in the pool all first half — treat it like a protected map.**
83.3% WR in Y10S4 is not an accident. If Coastline hasn't been in the ban conversation, it shouldn't need to be. Know your bests and keep them.

**3. Your Smoke reads are self-directed and that's a feature, not a bug.**
Don't let anyone else dictate when you pop canisters. Your WR validates it. The one coaching note: check in with the team after the round about timing so they can start anticipating it and playing off it rather than being surprised by it.

---

_Detailed operator tables, map splits, and full season archives are in your ALL_TIME.md, Y10S4.md, and Y11S1.md files._

# Krafty — Y11S1 Coaching Brief

_Season: Year 11 Season 1 — Operation Silent Hunt | Updated: 2026-03-07_

---

## Your Role

You're the newest player on the B Team and the one with the most room to grow. Your mechanics are there — a ~49% HS% career average is real aim. The gap right now is decision-making: when to peek, when to hold, when to let the situation come to you instead of forcing it. DEF is where you're already contributing. ATK is the development area. Y11S1 is about building a consistent foundation.

---

## What's Working

**Operators — DEF**
Your DEF cluster is working. Lesion was your anchor in Y10S4 at 81r / 63.0% WR, and Ela and Azami both posted 60%+ WR alongside it. All four passive trap ops (Lesion, Ela, Azami, Thorn) are producing. This is the right approach for where you're at — passive, trap-based, make the enemy come to you.

**Maps**
Clubhouse was your best map in Y10S4 (80% WR in 5 matches — most reliable sample). Villa hit 100% in 3 games. Both maps are in the current Y11S1 pool and both stay in for the first half. These are your confidence maps.

**DEF side consistency**
Across almost every map you played in Y10S4, your DEF round WR was significantly higher than your ATK round WR. That's a clear pattern. You play defense better than attack right now, and that's fine — it's a development priority, not a failure.

---

## Areas to Work On

**Operators — ATK**
Stop playing Zero. Your Y10S4 Zero K/D was 0.19 in 18 rounds — that's the lowest K/D on any op you played in meaningful volume. Zero isn't the problem, it's a symptom: fragmented ATK picks without clear purpose hurt your results. Maverick is your best ATK option (50% WR in 20r) and should lead your ATK volume in Y11S1.

**Decision-making / Peeking timing**
Your HS% is high, which means when you shoot at someone, you're hitting them. The K/D being below that suggests you're often in fights you didn't choose the right timing for — peeking into utility, pushing when the information says hold, or moving before the team is ready. The coaching focus is not your aim. It's asking: "should I be making this move right now?"

**Rank progression**
The Y11S1 development target is reaching Gold rank consistently. Bronze was Y10S4's result. Y11S1 should show improvement. If you're still in Silver/Bronze at the mid-season check, it's worth a direct conversation about what's holding the rank back.

---

## Y11S1 Priorities

**1. Maverick is your ATK identity. Commit to it.**
50% WR at 20 rounds is the best ATK result in your pool. Drop Zero. Drop Iana as the primary. Build your ATK pool around Maverick + a hard breach support (Hibana or Thermite). Volume and repetition will turn 50% into something higher.

**2. Lesion is your anchor. Trust it.**
Your DEF numbers across all passive trap ops are the strongest part of your game. Don't abandon the trap identity to experiment with aggressive ops before your ATK side is stable. Play Lesion on defense, place traps on entry points, and stay passive. The WR will follow.

**3. Ask yourself one question before every peek: "do I have information that says this is safe?"**
High HS% + low K/D almost always means peeking without enough justification. If the answer to that question is "no" — hold. The fight will come to you, especially on defense.

---

_Detailed operator tables, map data, and full season archives are in your ALL_TIME.md, Y10S4.md, and Y11S1.md files._

bTeam.coachingContent:
# Bob — Y11S1 Coaching Brief

_Season: Year 11 Season 1 — Operation Silent Hunt | Updated: 2026-03-07_

---

## Your Role

You're the B Team's DEF anchor. Kapkan first, every time. Your career DEF identity is established and it works — Kapkan at 71.1% WR in Y10S4, Frost and Caveira as strong depth picks. The problem to solve this season is on attack: your ATK pool has been inconsistent, and getting it stable is the one thing standing between your current form and what you're capable of.

---

## What's Working

**Operators — DEF**
Kapkan is your clearest asset at any level — 61.3% all-time WR in 483 rounds, and 71.1% in Y10S4. Always assign Kapkan first on defense. Frost follows close behind (62.7% WR / 1.28 K/D all-time). Aruni is a sleeper pick that showed 83.3% WR / 2.67 K/D in a small Y10S4 sample — worth expanding.

**Maps — Season start**
Kanal was your worst career map (35.5% WR in 31 career games) and it is **out of rotation all first half**. That's a significant advantage for your early-season WR. Oregon (59.4%, 32M) and Bank (58.6%, 29M) are two of your three best career maps — both are in the current pool. This is a good opening position.

**Early Y11S1 signs**
Brava is leading your ATK early. That's a more conventional pick than last season's Dokkaebi/Glaz/Denari approach. If Brava holds as your ATK anchor, that's a positive development.

---

## Areas to Work On

**Operators — ATK**
Y10S4 ATK pool (Nomad / Dokkaebi / Glaz / Gridlock / Zero) was fragmented and underperforming. The best ATK op you played was Zero at 1.00 K/D in 8r — that's the high bar. Thermite, Buck, Thatcher, Brava, and Ash are all more conventional and would likely serve you better. The direction is clear: simpler, more purpose-driven ATK picks.

**HS% monitoring**
Your career HS% is ~49%. In Y10S4 it dropped to 24.8% — a significant fall. If it doesn't recover toward your career average in the first 30+ ranked matches of Y11S1, that's worth addressing directly. High HS% suggests your mechanics are strong when you're on form; a major drop usually signals a slump or positioning change that's creating worse shot angles.

**Maps — Double weakness**
Clubhouse is 47.1% WR and 0.72 K/D all-time in 34 matches. Both your WR and K/D are weak there — that's a double weakness, not just a losing map. Nighthaven (30.8%) is also a genuine low. Have a plan when these maps come up rather than treating them as normal.

---

## Y11S1 Priorities

**1. Kanal is gone — use the first half to build momentum.**
You will not see your worst career map for the first half of Y11S1. Oregon and Bank are both available. This is your opportunity to build win rate and confidence before harder maps return mid-season. Take it.

**2. Commit to a conventional ATK identity.**
Pick two or three ops and stick with them: Brava + Kapkan crossover utility, or Thermite + Buck, or Thatcher support. The fragmented approach from Y10S4 (6+ different low-volume ATK picks) is what's hurting your attack side. Volume and repetition on fewer ops builds real performance.

**3. Watch your HS%.**
It's the cleanest indicator of whether you're on form. If it's recovering toward 40%+ in the first pulls of Y11S1, everything is fine. If it stays near 25%, bring it up — it's worth having a direct conversation about what's changed mechanically or in your crosshair placement.

---

_Detailed operator tables, map splits, and full season archives are in your ALL_TIME.md, Y10S4.md, and Y11S1.md files._

Despite this, K/D stays near 1.0 all-time, which suggests individual combat is fine — round wins on ATK come from better coordination, not aim.

**Role fit:** DEF anchor / intel operator. Caveira/Valkyrie are intel-gathering ops played at high WR. Aruni is a hard-anchor gadget op. Mira (24r, 70.8% WR) is a standout small-sample op worth developing — could be Hunter's best DEF pick with more reps.

## Identity

**Strengths:** DEF anchor with strong hard-denial pool. Bandit (67.4% WR), Frost (65.1%), Azami (63.0%), Castle (61.2%), Kaid (55.4%), Kapkan (57.9%) — multiple DEF ops above 55% WR with 30+ rounds. Consistent hard-anchor identity.

**Tendencies:** Breach-utility on ATK (Buck/Thatcher/Thermite high-volume) with below-average results. The raw K/D (0.65) undersells DEF contributions — utility placements, traps, and anchor positioning generate round wins that don't show up in K/D. Zero (70r, 45.7% WR, 0.72 K/D) is the most efficient ATK op and a better choice than Buck/Thatcher.

**Role fit:** Hard-anchor DEF specialist. Mira (11r, 72.7% WR, 1.14 K/D) is a standout small-sample stat — could develop into a primary Mira main. Fenrir (6r, 1.50 K/D) also noteworthy.

**Solo vs. Group:** Insufficient recent data. 307 career ranked matches shows experience. Best season was High Calibre (Y8S4): 44M, 2,884 MMR, 0.72 K/D. Recent seasons near-absent (2M in Y10S4, 0M in Y11S1).

## Career Coaching Notes

- **Buck/Thatcher ATK is high-volume but losing** — Buck 148r at 37.2% WR, Thatcher 97r at 36.1%. These ops are losing rounds consistently. Zero (45.7%) and Thermite (51.2%) are better ATK options by WR.
- **DEF is the genuine strength.** Bandit leading at 67.4% WR (43r) is rare for a non-main-stack player. Multiple DEF ops consistently above 60% — strong anchor identity.
- **Mira is the hidden gem** — 72.7% WR, 1.14 K/D in 11r. Worth developing. If D-Man can reliably play Mira, that's a legitimate stack asset on DEF.
- **Fenrir (6r, 1.50 K/D)** — tiny sample but the highest K/D of any op with 5+ rounds. Possible fragger upside on DEF.
